review_summary: The transcript provides enough grounded evidence to generate 2 reinforcement questions about future BCIs, inner-speech decoding, and neuroethics.

question_bank.question: Why is inner-speech decoding highlighted as an important future direction?
choices: Attempted-speech systems are still slower than natural conversation, so inner speech could support more effortless communication
Inner speech removes every ethical concern from BCIs
Inner speech needs no brain recordings
Attempted-speech systems already exceed natural speaking speed
question_bank.explanation: The motivation is speed and naturalness: current attempted-speech BCIs are impressive but still slower than ordinary conversation, so decoding inner speech could reduce effort and improve communication rate.

question_bank.question: Which concern is presented as a major ethical challenge for future inner-speech BCIs?
choices: They could reveal thoughts or memories that a person did not intend to express
They can work only with 3D avatars
They eliminate the need for consent procedures
They guarantee zero word error rate
question_bank.explanation: Inner-speech decoding raises special privacy and consent issues because the boundary between intended communication and private thought becomes much harder to protect.
